title: ginnania racemosa
intro: Ginnania racemosa, also known as the Indian tinospora or gurjungia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. Ginnania racemosa is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory and gastrointestinal infections, and improving liver and kidney function. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or powder, and can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
